---
title: "Drift Frequency Analysis"
author: "relay_42"
date: "2026-06-17"
---

## Overview

This Quarto document analyzes signal patterns from the R3L:F drift network.

### Correlation Matrix

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# Simulated drift signals
data = {
    'frequency': [0.1, 0.5, 1.2, 2.0, 0.8],
    'amplitude': [1.0, 0.7, 0.3, 0.9, 0.6],
    'ttl_hours': [168, 72, 24, 96, 120]
}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
corr = df.corr()
```

### Results

The correlation between frequency and TTL is **-0.42**, suggesting higher-frequency signals tend to have shorter lifespans.

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Sample count | 5 |
| Mean TTL | 96h |
| Std Dev | 52.5h |

### Visualizing the Network

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import networkx as nx

G = nx.Graph()
G.add_edge("relay_42", "signal_wolf", type="SYM")
G.add_edge("relay_42", "drift_runner", type="SYM")
G.add_edge("drift_runner", "echo_9", type="A-SYM")
```

> Files expire. Permanence is earned.
